programmes.show_description: The previously unobserved religious festival of San Lorenzo means no fun or alcohol is to be had anywhere in Benidorm, but the guests are determined to ignore it. Joyce has a different attitude. S9 E7
Terry is employed to look after the son of a crooked property tycoon whose shady past has caught up with him. S3 E8: The Son Also Rises
Alf Garnett is spurred into action by the arrival of his AIDS leaflet. S3 E2
George's car fails its MOT and he needs £20 to get it fixed - he sells his piano to Robin, but the problem is how to get it upstairs. S5 E2: Right Said George
David refuses to go with Amy to visit the ruins by saying that he already booked a court to play tennis with Linda; Robert still does not suspect, but Amy finally sees the evidence. S1 E6: Forty Love
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E7: Something in the Night
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E8: Lucky For Some
A trip to the heart of Cumbria with a journey from Whitehaven to Carlisle, with a stunning coastline overlooking the Irish Sea and calling in at several small Cumbrian towns and villages on the way.
Make perfect toasties right in your microwave - that's right! Clever Micro Toastie is microwave cooking with a crunch! Order now at www.highstreettv.com. Teleshopping.

Penny looks back at her relationship with Vince, from their first meeting to a disastrous wedding day and the chance meeting that brought them back together. Stars Jan Francis and Paul Nicholas. S2 E8
The workers of Hatley railway station live a gentle and predictable life, but that is rudely interrupted by some alarming news and a new station master. S1 E1: All Change
Harry the signalman has fattened up a lovely turkey in time for Christmas, but the turkey seems to have other plans. S1 E2: Talking Turkey
New station master Cecil Parkin is intent on making an impression at Hatley, but Jack is sure that he's already made an impression on his wife May. S1 E3: The Train Now Standing
The station staff are ready to help Cecil move into the station master's house, and May has a confession to make and shares it with Ethel... but will she tell Jack? S1 E4: A Moving Story
Harry Lambert has been on duty all night in the signal box when the delivery of a body causes problems - should it be classed as freight or passenger? S1 E5: The Late Mr Buckly
The finest comedy moments from Ronnie Barker. Featuring classic footage from his decades of TV work including The Two Ronnies and Porridge. With Ronnie Corbett and rare archive material.
Sharon's living back in her council flat and Tracey's still in Chigwell with younger son Travis, but what's become of Dorien and what can bring the Birds back together? S10 E1: Gimme Shelter
Sharon finds that looking after Dorien's every wish is a full-time job - with money tight, Sharon and Tracey take Dorien's designer clothes to a car boot sale to raise funds. S10 E2: Slave
Garth and Marcie aren't getting any 'together time' so it's all change in the bedrooms again - Sharon tries to bed down in the loft, but she makes an unexpected discovery. S10 E3: Hot Stuff
Tracey, Sharon and Dorien each get romantically involved with three very different men, and Travis has romantic troubles of his own. S10 E4: Hearts For Sale
Tracey becomes self-conscious about her appearance and has Botox, Travis gets a tattoo to impress a girl at school, and Sharon looks as if she'll need a hearing aid. S10 E5: Tattoo You
Sharon gets a visit from estranged husband Chris who is seeking a quick divorce so that he can marry his new love Angie - a blind woman - but Tracey has a few doubts. S10 E6: Blinded By Love
Sharon, Tracey and Dorien all lose their jobs and the resulting financial crisis leads to a huge row - some home truths are revealed and the three of them hit rock bottom. S10 E7: Back To Zero
Harold has nowhere to take his lady friends and resolves to get himself a bachelor pad, but his love life doesn't go according to plan; meanwhile, Albert has a new lease of life. S4 E5: A Box in Town
Harold has some big political ambitions and holds a local Labour Party meeting at home; Albert, a fervent Tory, sets out to disrupt it. S4 E6: My Old Man's a Tory
Albert is looking forward to another family Christmas, but Harold wants to go to Majorca and put his father in a home for the duration. Stars Harry H Corbett and Wilfrid Brambell. S7 E8: The Party
Arthur comes back to earth with a bump when he learns that he faces tough opposition for the Daley inheritance. S9 E12: A Taste of Money
Concerns from the Blow and Go team for Liam's wellbeing lead to confusion when they fear that he has joined a self-help group to confront his traumatic childhood, and Monty returns. S9 E8
The Maguire clan is fully mobilised for the christening of its newest member: baby Connor. Mimi is organising everything, including reallocating godmother duties, leaving Kelly devastated. S7 E2
While the flatmates compete to get the flat to themselves to entertain friends, George is trying to find jobs to avoid Mildred. S5 E4: Love and Let Love
An election polarises the household - Rigsby supports the Conservatives, Alan and Philip lean towards Labour, and Miss Jones is impressed with the Liberal candidate. S1 E7: Stand Up and Be Counted
Concerns from the Blow and Go team for Liam's wellbeing lead to confusion when they fear that he has joined a self-help group to confront his traumatic childhood, and Monty returns. S9 E8
Terry agrees to accompany Barry the Bookie to hand some winnings over to a punter, but he realises too late that he's been caught up in a scam. S3 E9: Why Pay Tax
When Alf Garnett is given a cordless telephone it turns out to be more of a hindrance than a help. S3 E3
George needs help finding the answers for all the competitions he enters - luckily, Robin is starting a new job selling encyclopaedias and is looking for his first buyer. S5 E3: A Little Knowledge
With their affair now exposed, Linda and David plan to run off together - Robert wants to fight David, but Amy decides to foil their plan. S1 E7: Hasta La Vista
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E1: For Better, For Worse
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E2: A Place in the Sun
Llangollen Railway was started in 1975 by a group of enthusiasts who saw the potential for a scenic heritage line through the Dee Valley from Llangollen for ten miles to the town of Corwen.

Jet Hawk is cordless, rechargeable and turns any water source into a powerful pressure washer. Call or visit highstreettv.com for more details. Teleshopping.
Mattresses and toppers from Dormeo - the home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget. Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
When Mildred decides that she and George will celebrate their anniversary at a posh London hotel whatever the cost, a shady businessman mistakes George for a hitman. Comedy 1980 (U)
A horsebox belonging to Lady Lawrence is delivered to the station - and Wilfred is on hand with the essentials - while Harry's signal box seems to be on fire. S1 E6: Horse Play
The staff are up early to greet a new arrival at Hatley Station - another arrival later in the day is less welcome... S1 E7: Past Love
May is keen to make a career move to a bigger station, but both Jack and Cecil are set against her going and both try to apply for a transfer to be near her. S1 E8: Job Opportunity
Jack and May have problems with their teenage daughter, and it's dance night and Ethel is excited about hoping to find love at last. S1 E9: Sleeping Around
Classic sitcom about walking disaster Frank Spencer (Michael Crawford). Frank and Betty are looking forward to Christmas, especially the church's Nativity play. Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em: S2 E7
Classic sitcom starring Michael Crawford as the hapless Frank Spencer. As Christmas approaches, Frank takes just one more lesson before yet another driving test. Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em: S2 E8
Frank Spencer's grandfather pays for flying lessons so that Frank can help on his Australian sheep farm. Betty doesn't think it's a good idea, nor does the flying instructor. Stars Michael Crawford.
Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms; he's happy Miss Jones has returned but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy
Rigsby attempts to have himself cast as male lead in a play written by effeminate lodger Hilary when he discovers that Ruth is playing the role of the love interest. S3 E2: Stage Struck
Rigsby turns up at the house with a new sports car apparently purchased in yet another attempt to woo Miss Jones, but his driving doesn't appear to be as stylish as his motor. S3 E3: Clunk Click
Rigsby is soon calling the Samaritans to stop a suicide attempt when a new lodger arrives at the house, but which of the residents will come off worse? S3 E4: The Good Samaritans
When Miss Jones raises suspicions about new tenant Madeline, Rigsby is not too bothered about her brand of 'modern interpretive dancing' but isn't that keen on her partner. S3 E5: Fawcett's Python
Alan's getting serious about Caroline, but her parents are well-to-do and Rigsby is far from convinced that the young scruff could possibly make the relationship work. S3 E6: The Cocktail Hour
Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)
Sharon and Tracey get a glimpse of a possible future when they see two elderly sisters in the supermarket, and Dorien's court case comes to a conclusion. S10 E8: You Can't Always Get What You Want
Dorien is treating Sharon and Tracey to a Christmas trip to the Canary Islands; with an overdramatic passenger also on board, the plane does not reach its destination. S11 E1: Birds on a Plane
Penniless and facing the threat of deportation from Australia, Ray and Arthur must try to raise funds for a return airfare home. S9 E13: For a Few Dollars More
When Baby Jesus contaminates the swimming pool with weedkiller, the guests have to be evacuated. Joyce hastily arranges a trip to the circus, and helps Mateo round up some performers. S9 E9
Karen's obsession with proving that she's more than just a mum worries Jamie. Liam's pursuit of true love is threatened by his own lies. And Maxine is in turmoil when faced with a choice. S7 E3

When Mildred enters a flower arranging competition, Chrissy offers her a plant from her garden; when Larry sees one in Chrissy's flat, he thinks it's a cannabis plant. S5 E5: How Does Your Garden Grow
When Ruth is depressed following the 'deception' of a recent male friend, Rigsby takes what he believes to be his chance to woo her. S2 E1: The Permissive Society
